title: AI Data-Center Power — Sector Pack
markdown:
# AI Data-Center Power — Sector Pack

**The bottleneck for AI compute has shifted from chips to electrons. Hyperscalers are now contracting multi-GW blocks of nuclear and gas years ahead of need, and the scarce asset is permitted, large-block, shovel-ready power — exactly what Fermi is selling.**

## Market size & demand

AI data-center buildout is driving an unprecedented power-procurement cycle. The largest hyperscalers are collectively expected to deploy on the order of **$600B toward GPU and data-center capacity by 2026**, and the gating constraint is increasingly electricity, not silicon. Demand is being met through two channels in parallel: **nuclear** for clean baseload at scale, and **natural gas** for speed-to-market (deployable in ~2–4 years, better aligned with aggressive buildout timelines than new nuclear).

## Structural tailwinds

- **Power scarcity is structural, not cyclical.** Grid interconnection queues stretch years; large contiguous blocks of firm power are the rate-limiter on AI expansion.
- **Hyperscalers are contracting directly.** Meta signed deals with Oklo, Vistra, and TerraPower for up to **6.6 GW** of nuclear by 2035; Amazon's **$18B, 1.9 GW** Talen/Susquehanna PPA (June 2025) set the template; Microsoft underwrote the restart of Three Mile Island Unit 1 (~835 MW) via Constellation.
- **Behind-the-meter campuses** that co-locate generation with compute bypass the interconnection queue entirely — the Fermi model.
- **Permits and land are the moat.** A Clean Air Permit and shovel-ready acreage are years of optionality that capital alone can't replicate quickly.

## Structural headwinds

- **Tenant concentration risk.** A handful of hyperscalers are the entire demand curve; losing one bid can strand a project.
- **Financing depends on contracts.** Non-recourse project finance requires signed offtake — pre-contract developers carry refinancing and dilution risk.
- **Construction & schedule risk.** Turbine lead times, cooling-system design, and NRC timelines routinely slip.
- **Policy & rate exposure.** Permitting regimes and the cost of capital swing project economics materially.

## Competitive landscape (public comps)

| Company | Ticker | Role | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| Vistra | VST | IPP / nuclear+gas fleet | 2.1+ GW to Meta; cash-flowing fleet |
| Constellation Energy | CEG | Largest US nuclear operator | TMI restart for Microsoft |
| Talen Energy | TLN | Merchant nuclear+gas | $18B AWS Susquehanna PPA template |
| Oklo | OKLO | SMR developer | 1.2 GW Pike County, OH for Meta; pre-revenue |
| GE Vernova | GEV | Turbine / grid equipment | Picks-and-shovels to the buildout |
| Digital Realty / Equinix | DLR / EQIX | Data-center REITs | Demand side; power-constrained |

Fermi sits between the IPP fleet operators (cash-flowing, signed PPAs) and the pre-revenue developers (OKLO) — it has permits and a pipeline but no executed anchor lease, which is why it trades at a steep discount to the operating names.

## Investment angles (prioritized)

1. **Anchor-tenant conversion is the binary.** Fermi's entire re-rating hinges on signing a hyperscaler. The OpenAI read-through (Citizens, $30 PT) shows how violently the stock moves on any credible signal — but the catalyst remains third-party inference, not ink.
2. **Behind-the-meter is the durable structural winner.** Whoever can deliver large-block firm power without waiting on the interconnection queue captures premium economics. Fermi's ~6 GW air permit and Texas Tech ground lease are the relevant moat.
3. **Picks-and-shovels (GEV, turbine/electrical suppliers) offer lower-variance exposure** to the same demand wave without single-tenant binary risk.

## Sources

1. Utility Dive — "Meta inks nuclear deals for up to 6.6 GW from Oklo, Vistra, TerraPower" · 2026
2. TechCrunch — "Meta signs deals with three nuclear companies for 6-plus GW of power" · Jan 9, 2026
3. enkiai.com — "AWS Nuclear, 17-Year Talen Energy PPA" ($18B / 1.9 GW Susquehanna) · 2025–26
4. CarbonCredits.com — "AI Demand to Drive $600B From the Big Five... by 2026" · 2026
5. ETF Stream — "Data centres drive momentum for nuclear and North American natural gas" · 2026
6. StockTitan — Fermi Project Matador milestones (6 GW air permit; 2 GW secured) · Jun 2026

*Not investment advice.*
